Idiomatic Expressions

  • 公視之 - 公 refers to an official or public figure, 視之 means to observe or look at someone.
  • 花不屑妝，柳不胜衣 - 花不屑妝 suggests natural beauty without makeup, 柳不胜衣 implies a slender figure that cannot bear the weight of clothing.
  • 一妙龄绝色人也 - A young woman of extraordinary beauty, emphasizing her youth and exceptional appearance.
  • 公大悦 - The official was greatly pleased, indicating a strong positive reaction.
  • 伪言口渴，遣小奚丐茶 - 伪言 means pretend to say, 口渴 means thirsty, 遣小奚丐茶 means send a little servant to beg for tea, a polite way of asking for something.
  • 兼诘其姓氏 - And ask about her name and family, where 诘 means to inquire or ask.
  • 小奚将公命 - The little servant carries out the official's command, where 将 means to carry out or execute.
  • 篱边人笑曰 - The person by the fence laughed and said, where 篱边人 refers to someone standing near a fence.
  • 个老子口无停声 - This old man is always talking, where 个老子 is a colloquial term for an old man, and 口无停声 means never stops talking.
  • 予家亦无闲炉灶，烹苦茗以待子不时之需 - My home also has no spare stove, so I brew bitter tea to meet your occasional needs, where 烹苦茗 means to brew bitter tea, and 不时之需 means occasional needs.
  • 予不复记忆 - I no longer remember, where 不复 means no longer, and 记忆 means to remember.
  • 我父母容或知之 - My parents might know, where 容或 means possibly or perhaps, and 知之 means to know.
  • 即呼曰 - Then called out, where 即 means immediately or then, and 呼曰 means to call out or shout.
  • 阿母！我家亦如人有姓耶？ - Mother! Does my family have a surname like other people? where 阿母 is a term of endearment for mother, and 如人有姓 means like other people have surnames.
  • 渠来问我，我则无由知 - When he comes to ask me, I have no way of knowing, where 渠 means he, and 无由知 means have no way of knowing.
  • 公不禁大噱 - The official couldn't help but burst into laughter, where 不禁 means couldn't help, and 大噱 means burst into laughter.
  • 童亦粲然 - The child also smiled brightly, where 童 means child, and 粲然 means smiling brightly.
  • 有顷，见一媪出 - After a while, an old woman came out, where 有顷 means after a while, and 媪 means old woman.
  • 荆布修洁 - Wearing simple and clean clothes, where 荆布 refers to simple clothing made of coarse fabric, and 修洁 means clean and tidy.
  • 盖其母也 - It is her mother, where 盖 is a particle used to introduce a conclusion or explanation, and 其母 means her mother.
  • 问客何来 - Ask the guest where he comes from, where 问 means to ask, 客 means guest, and 何来 means where he comes from.
  • 幸勿见哂 - Please do not laugh at me, where 幸 means please, 勿 means do not, 见 means to, and 哂 means to laugh at.
  • 公因致揖 - The official then bowed, where 因 means then, 致 means to perform, and 揖 means to bow.
  • 亲叩其里族 - Personally asked about her hometown and clan, where 亲 means personally, 叩 means to ask, and 里族 means hometown and clan.
  • 则王姓，夫故农业，室甚贫 - She is surnamed Wang, her husband is a farmer, and their family is very poor, where 则 means then, 王姓 means surnamed Wang, 夫 means husband, 故农业 means is a farmer, and 室甚贫 means their family is very poor.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 妙龄绝色 - Refers to a young woman of extraordinary beauty, a common theme in classical Chinese literature.
  • 荆布修洁 - Describes simple and clean clothing, reflecting the modest lifestyle of the characters.
  • 里族 - Refers to the hometown and clan, important social and familial ties in traditional Chinese society.

Proper Nouns

  • 小奚 - Refers to a little servant.
  • 媪 - Refers to an old woman, specifically the mother in this context.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 因而百夕之中，僅得以遂 - Thus, within a hundred nights, only just succeeded. '因而' indicates a causal relationship, and '僅得以遂' suggests the goal was barely achieved.
  • 不以為歉 - Not considering it a regret. A common classical expression indicating no remorse or regret.
  • 公得以颐养 - '颐养' is a classical term for 'to support and care for in old age.'
  • 戚族毕贺 - '戚族' refers to close family and extended clan members.
  • 家人亦无不称觞 - '称觞' is a classical expression for toasting or offering a drink in celebration.
  • 女不知庆 - The daughter did not know how to celebrate. An idiomatic way of saying the daughter did not participate in the celebration.
  • 独茹素弥月 - '茹素' means to eat only vegetarian food, often used in a religious or devotional context.
  • 愿公再延一纪 - '一纪' is a classical term for a period of twelve years.
  • 无敢奢望 - Dared not hope for more. A modest way of expressing a wish.
  • 公病益剧，竟不起 - '益剧' means to become more severe, and '竟不起' indicates that he did not recover.
  • 女忽辞公欲行 - '辞公' is a formal way of saying 'to bid farewell to the elder.'
  • 疑其有异志 - Suspected she had strange intentions. A classical way of expressing suspicion.
  • 询以焉往 - '焉往' is a classical expression meaning 'where to go.'
  • 愿先公行，为公驱狐狸于地下 - A metaphorical expression, likely meaning that the daughter wishes to accompany her father in the afterlife and protect him from supernatural beings.
  • 言已，顿仆于地，七孔殷然 - '七孔殷然' is a classical expression describing a violent death or a dramatic, tragic end.
  • 则已饮鸩半日矣 - '饮鸩' is a classical idiom for drinking poison, often used metaphorically, but here it is literal.
  • 公太息许时，复大笑曰 - '太息' is a classical term for a long sigh, and '许时' means for some time.
  • 若不负我，我何悲为？ - A rhetorical question expressing relief or acceptance.
  • 亟呼诸郎君至，遗命以女尸合葬 - '遗命' is a classical term for a last will or command.
  • 瞬息亦卒 - '瞬息' means in an instant, and '卒' is a classical term for dying.
  • 诸郎君克成父志，不敢有违 - '克成' means to accomplish or fulfill, and '不敢有违' means dared not disobey.
  • 女之名与公皆不朽 - '不朽' means 'immortal' or 'unforgettable.'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 吴氏之茔 - The tomb of the Wu family. Likely refers to the burial site of a noble or influential family.
  • 痴狐墓 - A metaphorical name for the daughter's tomb. In Chinese culture, 'fox' can symbolize a spirit, a supernatural being, or a devoted and loyal companion. '痴狐' implies a 'foolish fox,' likely a metaphor for the daughter's self-sacrificial devotion and purity.
  • 饮鸩 - To drink poison. A classical idiom that can be used both literally and metaphorically. Here it is used literally.
  • 合葬 - To be buried together. A traditional Chinese practice where spouses or close family members are buried together, signifying love, loyalty, and unity in death.
  • 七孔殷然 - Blood gushing from seven orifices. A classical description of a violent or dramatic death, often associated with tragic or supernatural stories.
  • 太息 - To sigh deeply. A classical expression used to convey deep emotion, often in the context of sorrow, regret, or relief.

Proper Nouns

  • 女 - The daughter. A general term for a female, used here to refer to the daughter of the elder.
  • 吴氏 - The Wu family. A proper noun referring to the family name of the elder and the daughter.
  • 痴狐 - Foolish fox. A metaphor for the daughter's devotion and purity.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 隨園老人曰 - Formal opening meaning 'Master Suīyuán says,' where '隨園' (Suīyuán) is the name of the author or a pseudonym.
  • 吳太仆立朝所行者，猶在人意中 - Means 'What Wu Taifu did in his official career was still within people's expectations.' '立朝' refers to serving in the court or holding office.
  • 今其如夫人，迥出入意表 - Translates to 'Now, his concubines, however, greatly exceeded people's expectations.' '如夫人' refers to concubines or secondary wives, and '迥出入意表' is an idiomatic expression meaning 'far beyond expectations' or 'surprisingly different from what was anticipated.'
  • 孰謂巾帼中竟無人榮及夫子 - Translates to 'Who would have thought that among women there were none who brought honor to their husbands?' '巾帼' refers to women, and '榮及夫子' means 'bringing honor to one's husband.'
  • 燈下美人 - A poetic title meaning 'Beautiful Woman Under the Lamp,' likely referring to a story or anecdote about a woman seen in the light of a lamp.
  • 瓊州餘舜章，少時讀書於某寺 - Means 'Yu Shunchang from Qióngzhōu, in his youth, studied at a certain temple.' '瓊州' is the name of a place, and '餘舜章' is the name of the protagonist.
  • 每當風清月白之頃，輒有良夜如何之慨 - Translates to 'Whenever the wind was clear and the moon bright, he would feel a sense of how wonderful the night was.' '風清月白' describes a serene, beautiful night, and '良夜如何之慨' expresses a feeling of appreciation for the beauty of the night.
  • 忽燭光屍閃如人，意為寺僧 - Means 'Suddenly, the candlelight flickered like a person, and he thought it was a monk from the temple.' '屍閃' is a colloquial term for flickering or flashing, and '寺僧' refers to monks from the temple.
  • 余心疑，掩卷伺之 - Translates to 'I was suspicious, so I closed the book and waited to see what would happen.' '心疑' means 'to be suspicious,' and '掩卷' means 'to close the book.'
  • 又許時，其狀若煙而慘淡，倏有倏無 - Means 'After a while, its appearance was like smoke, dim and flickering, sometimes present, sometimes absent.' '許時' means 'after a while,' and '倏有倏無' describes something appearing and disappearing rapidly.
  • 余素以膽力自雄，審知為鬼物，益伺之 - Translates to 'I have always prided myself on my courage, and after careful consideration, I realized it was a ghost, so I waited even more intently.' '素以膽力自雄' means 'to always pride oneself on one's courage,' and '審知' means 'to realize after careful consideration.'
  • 久之，較前益真，但具體而微 - Means 'After a long time, it became more real than before, but still small and vague.' '較前益真' means 'more real than before,' and '具體而微' means 'small but detailed.'
  • 又久之，甫露半面 - Translates to 'After a long time, it finally revealed half of its face.' '甫露半面' means 'finally revealing half of its face.'
  • 至夜分，全形現矣 - Means 'By midnight, its full form appeared.' '夜分' refers to midnight, and '全形現矣' means 'its full form appeared.'
  • 余亟視之，綠鬢翹然，紅姿綽約，美人也 - Translates to 'I quickly looked at it, and saw a woman with green hair that stood up, and a red, graceful figure—she was a beautiful woman.' '綠鬢翹然' describes her hair, and '紅姿綽約' describes her figure.

  • 如夫人 - Refers to concubines or secondary wives, a term used in ancient China to describe women who were not the primary wife but were still part of the household.

  • 盖缘洞房有待，虽庶士不免摽梅之叹耳 - Phrase meaning 'it is because of the waiting in the bridal chamber, even common scholars cannot avoid the sighs of the bai mei (a metaphor for a woman of marriageable age).'
  • 妾有良策，故来敬陈于郎君 - Phrase meaning 'I have a good strategy, so I have come to respectfully present it to the young man.'

  • 欲速则速之，早尔数年，婚嫁固不妨也 - Phrase meaning 'if you want to hurry, then hurry, even several years earlier, marriage and wedding are not a problem.'
  • 余闻之，色甚喜 - Phrase meaning 'I heard it, and my face showed great joy.'
  • 折节求之 - Phrase meaning 'lowered my standards to seek it.'

  • 援琴之挑，良非君子所宜，妾亦不敢于导引 - Phrase meaning 'the act of playing the qin to entice, is not suitable for a gentleman, I also dare not to guide.'
  • 然郎君之妇，非始生三岁者 - Phrase meaning 'but the wife of the young man, not a three-year-old girl.'
  • 欲速则速之，早尔数年，婚嫁固不妨也 - Phrase meaning 'if you want to hurry, then hurry, even several years earlier, marriage and wedding are not a problem.'
  • 余又细询其说 - Phrase meaning 'I then asked her in detail about her plan.'
  • 美人笑曰 - Phrase meaning 'the beautiful woman laughed and said.'
  • 郎君勿絮絮，第置此于枕函，若所聘者，即至矣 - Phrase meaning 'young man, do not talk too much, just place this in your pillow box, if the one you are engaged to, will come immediately.'
  • 径置符于床头，倏忽如烟而逝 - Phrase meaning 'she directly placed the talisman on the bedhead, and suddenly disappeared like smoke.'
  • 甫就枕衾，即梦其父母为卜吉期，遣使亲迎 - Phrase meaning 'as soon as I lay down, I immediately dreamed that his parents were choosing a lucky date and sending an envoy to personally welcome him.'

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 余生之父母 - Metaphorical expression meaning 'parents who saved my life' or 'those who gave me a second chance.'
  • 悒悒 - Term expressing deep sorrow or anxiety.
  • 形神精魄，咸为所耗 - Literally 'body, spirit, essence, and soul are all consumed,' indicating severe physical and mental exhaustion.
  • 渐以痿惫 - Means 'gradually becoming weak and exhausted.'
  • 遂终日昏昏皆梦矣 - Means 'so that I am now constantly in a daze, living in a dream-like state.'
  • 强起如市 - Means 'forcing myself to go out into the marketplace,' implying a reluctant but necessary action.
  • 仅作壁上观 - Metaphorical expression meaning 'merely watching as if standing on a wall,' indicating passive onlookers who do not intervene.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 余 - First-person pronoun 'I' or 'me.'
  • 被殴者 - Refers to the person who was beaten.
  • 青蚨 - Ancient term for money or currency, often used in a poetic or metaphorical sense.
  • 葬父无资 - Refers to the lack of funds for a father's burial, a significant issue in traditional Chinese culture.
  • 小妻 - Refers to a secondary wife or concubine.
  • 悍不肯还 - Means 'stubbornly refusing to return,' indicating unwillingness to repay a debt.
  • 解 - Means 'to resolve' or 'to mediate.'
  • 事关节孝 - Refers to a critical point in filial piety, suggesting a moral or ethical dilemma.

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • The sentence '余生之父母，亦无如何，余亦为之悒悒，而梦则未绝也' is a series of clauses that express the narrator's feelings and state of mind.
  • The clause '形神精魄，咸为所耗，渐以痿惫，遂终日昏昏皆梦矣' describes the narrator's physical and mental state, emphasizing the gradual deterioration.
  • The clause '一日强起如市，见斗者二人，一人被殴，几殆，环如堵墙者，仅作壁上观，竟无人能为之解' describes the narrator's encounter with a violent scene and the passive onlookers.
  • The clause '余见不忍，前诘其由' shows the narrator's reaction to the scene and his attempt to understand the cause.
  • The clause '殴者素识余，忿忿言曰' introduces the aggressor and his reaction to the narrator.
  • The clause '渠售妇与予为小妻，既而悔之，所欠者又悍不肯还，故殴之，以泄吾忿' explains the aggressor's reasons for the violence.
  • The clause '余询被殴者：“值几何？”答曰：“因葬父无资，只贷渠青蚨五贯耳。妇不忍别，日涕泣惟愿死，谁赖渠也？”' details the victim's response and the underlying issues.
  • The clause '余踌躇自念，事关节孝，当思有以保全之' shows the narrator's internal conflict and his decision to act.

  • 卿等欲狂不能耳 - '卿等' is a respectful term for 'you all' or 'you guys,' and '欲狂不能耳' means 'you want to be wild but can't,' implying a playful or teasing tone.

  • 邂逅相逢，未敢轻泄 - '邂逅' means 'chance encounter,' '相逢' means 'meet,' and '未敢轻泄' means 'not daring to speak lightly,' suggesting that the speaker is cautious or reserved.

  • 知夫怒，无敢言 - Knowing the husband was angry, she dared not speak, where '无敢' means 'dared not.'

  • 窃思俟夫寝 - She secretly thought to wait until the husband slept, where '窃思' means 'secretly thought' or 'privately considered.'

  • 纤毫毕鉴 - Every detail was visible, where '纤毫' means 'every detail,' and '毕鉴' means 'all visible.'
  • 匿其足 - Hide her feet, where '匿' means 'hide.'
  • 佯笑曰 - Laughed falsely, where '佯' means 'falsely' or 'feigned.'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 姑 - Refers to the grandmother or mother-in-law.
  • 妇 - Refers to the wife.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 亦笑曰 - Conversational phrase meaning 'also laughed and said,' indicating a lighthearted or playful tone.
  • 若孜孜凝视 - Formal expression meaning 'if you are staring intently,' used to describe the husband's focused gaze.
  • 岂有人跣而行耶 - Rhetorical question meaning 'is there anyone who walks barefoot?' used to mock the husband's suspicion.
  • 遽曳其右足 - Vivid phrase meaning 'quickly pulled his right foot,' describing the husband's sudden action.
  • 大惭，低鬟无语 - Descriptive phrase meaning 'greatly ashamed, she lowered her head in silence,' capturing the wife's emotional state.
  • 谩骂曰 - Colloquial phrase meaning 'screamed and cursed,' indicating the husband's angry outburst.
  • 不从我言，致出予丑 - Harsh statement meaning 'because you didn't listen to me, you've made me look foolish,' expressing the husband's frustration.
  • 虽醢汝身，不足泄忿矣 - Hyperbolic phrase meaning 'even if I cut you into pieces, it wouldn't be enough to vent my anger,' emphasizing the husband's rage.
  • 固诘其履 - Formal expression meaning 'firmly questioned her about the shoes,' indicating the husband's insistence.
  • 体已冰矣 - Vivid phrase meaning 'her body was already cold,' describing the wife's state after death.
  • 大怖且悔 - Descriptive phrase meaning 'greatly terrified and regretful,' capturing the husband's emotional state after the wife's death.
  • 因断其缳，负之出户，投诸邻寺井中 - Detailed action sequence meaning 'he then cut the rope, carried her out of the room, and threw her into the well of a nearby temple,' describing the husband's disposal of the wife's body.
  • 喃喃登榻，怒骂不休 - Vivid phrase meaning 'muttering to himself, he climbed onto the bed and continued to curse,' capturing the husband's ongoing anger.
  • 竟缒帛于梁而自缢 - Detailed action sequence meaning 'she eventually tied a rope to the beam and hanged herself,' describing the wife's suicide.
  • 不及见母，竟往岳家逆妇 - Narrative phrase meaning 'not seeing his mother, he went to his father-in-law's house to take his wife,' indicating the husband's actions after the wife's death.
  • 翁媪言已送归，婿力白其无 - Narrative phrase meaning 'the old man and woman said she had already been sent back, and the son-in-law strongly denied it,' describing the confusion and denial.
  • 前送女者，适以事他出，共疑之，具控于官 - Narrative phrase meaning 'the person who had sent the daughter away had just gone out for some reason, and they all suspected him, filing a complaint with the authorities,' indicating the accusation and investigation.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 跣 - Refers to walking barefoot, a common practice in ancient China, but here used to mock the husband's suspicion.
  • 履 - Refers to shoes, a symbol of propriety and social standing in ancient China.
  • 室 - Refers to a wife, emphasizing the husband's disappointment and anger.
  • 醢 - A severe form of punishment involving dismemberment and cooking the body, used here hyperbolically to express the husband's rage.
  • 缳 - Refers to a rope or noose, used in the context of suicide.
  • 邻寺 - Refers to a nearby temple, a common place for disposal of bodies in ancient China.
  • 岳家 - Refers to the father-in-law's house, indicating the husband's visit to his wife's family.
  • 官 - Refers to the authorities, indicating the legal process that follows the accusation.

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • The rhetorical question '若孜孜凝视，岂有人跣而行耶' is used to mock the husband's suspicion, but it can be read as a direct challenge to his reasoning.
  • The husband's sudden action and question '遽曳其右足曰：‘是何独无？’' can be read as a continuation of his suspicion or as a new line of questioning.
  • The hyperbolic statement '虽醢汝身，不足泄忿矣' can be read as an expression of the husband's rage or as a threat to the wife.
  • The description of the wife's cold body and the husband's terror and regret '体已冰矣，大怖且悔' can be read as a single emotional moment or as two separate reactions.
  • The detailed action sequence '因断其缳，负之出户，投诸邻寺井中' can be read as a single, continuous action or as a series of separate steps.

Could it be that the golden rope has broken, causing the vase to remain stuck? where '大师何作此态' means 'Master, why are you in this state?' and '岂金绳中断，以致净瓶不出耶' means 'could it be that the golden rope has broken, causing the vase to remain stuck?'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 寧有慈航普渡，而高居彼岸者乎 - Rhetorical question using the Buddhist metaphor of '慈航普渡' (compassionate ferrying to the other shore) to imply that true compassion involves helping others reach enlightenment, not just remaining on the shore oneself.
  • 素能浚井，予缒汝下，渠乃可上 - Metaphorical expression where '浚井' (digging a well) represents preparation or effort, and '缒汝下' (lowering a rope to you) symbolizes assistance.
  • 遂浼少年执绠，已乃缒绳而下 - '浼' (to persuade or beg) and '执绠' (holding the rope) describe the act of persuading a young man to help by holding the rope.
  • 睨之，虽衣裾沾濡，貌颇婉丽 - '睨之' (glancing at her) and '貌颇婉丽' (her appearance was quite graceful and beautiful) describe the speaker's reaction to the woman's appearance.
  • 绐之曰 - '绐' (to deceive or trick) indicates that the speaker is lying to the woman.
  • 解绠束其纤腰 - '解绠' (untying the rope) and '束其纤腰' (tying it around her slender waist) describe the act of securing the woman with the rope.
  • 少年虑其复活，再取他石连投之，寂然无声 - '虑其复活' (fearing she might revive) and '寂然无声' (silence) describe the young man's actions and the result.
  • 妇见僧毙，知非好相识，大惧欲走 - '妇见僧毙' (the woman sees the monk die) and '知非好相识' (knows he was not a good acquaintance) describe the woman's reaction.
  • 少年胁之以力，不能脱，始勉从之 - '胁之以力' (threatening her with force) and '始勉从之' (finally reluctantly following him) describe the young man's coercion and the woman's compliance.
  • 曲折里许，至一土室中 - '曲折里许' (winding for about a mile) and '至一土室中' (reaching a cave) describe the journey and destination.
  • 僧与予言，意颇不善 - '意颇不善' (his intentions were not good) describes the monk's intentions.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 慈航普渡 - A Buddhist concept referring to the compassionate ferrying of beings to the other shore (enlightenment).
  • 浚井 - A metaphor for preparation or effort.
  • 缒绳而下 - A metaphor for assistance or rescue.

Idiomatic Expressions

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 僧 - Refers to a Buddhist monk, suggesting a religious context.
  • 涉訟 - Implies the legal system of the time, likely the imperial court or local magistrate.

  • 新樂 - Place name, likely a county or town in ancient China. Should be transliterated as 'Xinle.'
  • 傭 - Reflects the social structure of the time, where laborers were common and often from lower classes.
  • 旋里 - Common expression in classical Chinese, indicating a return to one’s hometown or family.
  • 妻 - Direct term for a married woman, and the relationship is formalized through the act of taking her as a wife.
  • 履 - In classical Chinese, *履* is a common term for a shoe, often used in both literal and metaphorical contexts.
  • 丐 - Implies a social hierarchy where one person is in a position to request something from another.
  • 钥 - The act of locking and unlocking doors is a common motif in classical Chinese narrative, symbolizing control and security.

Proper Nouns

  • 雀屏选 - Phoenix screen selection, a metaphor for a high standard of selection, often used in the context of choosing a spouse.
  • 师御穷之言 - The teacher's prediction of poverty, referring to a previous statement or prophecy about her future financial status.

Proper Nouns

  • 艳梅 - Yan Mei, the main character.
  • 师 - The teacher who made the prediction.

[602]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 居无何 - Temporal expression meaning 'after a short while' or 'in no time.'
  • 贺者盈闾 - Literally 'those who come to congratulate fill the gate,' meaning that many people have come to celebrate.
  • 戚族之妇莫不来 - 'Relatives and in-laws, no woman fails to come,' indicating that all family members have arrived.
  • 陆母酬酢已毕 - 'Madam Lu has finished the toasts and responses,' referring to the completion of formal social interactions.
  • 乃命新妇代为娱宾 - 'She then orders the new bride to entertain the guests,' where '新妇' refers to the newly married wife.
  • 艳梅称觞至一媪 - 'Yan Mei presents a cup of wine to an old woman,' where '称觞' means to present a cup of wine.
  • 年颇高大 - 'Her age is quite advanced,' meaning she is very old.
  • 昏愦龙钟 - Describes someone who is confused and feeble, often used for the elderly.
  • 太匆忙 - 'Too hasty' or 'too rushed.'
  • 明日又阿婿生辰 - 'Tomorrow is also the birthday of my son-in-law,' where '阿婿' refers to the son-in-law.
  • 虽无贺客，中馈亦岂能无事 - 'Even without guests, the kitchen still needs to be attended to,' where '中馈' refers to the kitchen or household affairs.
  • 语未已，座中多怒之以目 - 'Before she finished speaking, many in the audience glared at her,' where '座中' means 'in the audience.'
  • 艳梅心疑，然亦未敢研诘 - 'Yan Mei was suspicious, but dared not inquire further.'
  • 及夕入室，坚叩学洙 - 'At dusk, she entered the room and firmly questioned Xuezhu,' where '坚叩' means to firmly question.
  • 学洙以佳偶已谐，且因爱不能讳，微露其情 - 'Xuezhu explained that the good match had been made, and because of his love, he could not hide it, so he revealed the situation slightly.'
  • 则媒氏以斧柯无成，徒劳跋涉，因窃与陆母计，重赂日者，择古人全福之造，伪为赝鼎以往耳 - 'The matchmaker, seeing that the match had not been successful, wasted his efforts, and secretly plotted with Madam Lu, offering a large bribe to a fortune-teller, choosing an ancient person with complete good fortune, and pretending to be a fake treasure to deceive them.'
  • 艳梅大戚，亟索其真者 - 'Yan Mei became very sad and urgently demanded the truth.'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 贺者盈闾 - A common practice in traditional Chinese weddings where guests come to congratulate the newlyweds.
  • 戚族之妇 - Refers to relatives and in-laws, emphasizing the importance of family in traditional Chinese society.
  • 陆母 - The mother of the groom, often a respected figure in the family.
  • 新妇 - The newly married wife, who is expected to entertain guests.
  • 称觞 - Presenting a cup of wine, a traditional gesture of respect and celebration.
  • 昏愦龙钟 - Describes the elderly, often used to highlight their confusion or frailty.
  • 中馈 - Refers to the kitchen or household affairs, emphasizing the role of women in managing the household.
  • 媒氏 - The matchmaker, a crucial figure in traditional Chinese marriage arrangements.
  • 斧柯无成 - A metaphor for a failed match, where '斧柯' refers to a tool used in matchmaking.
  • 重赂日者 - Offering a large bribe to a fortune-teller, reflecting the superstition and reliance on fortune-telling in traditional Chinese culture.
  • 赝鼎 - A fake treasure, used metaphorically to describe a false or deceptive person.

Proper Nouns

  • 艳梅 - The name of the new bride, Yan Mei.
  • 陆母 - The mother of the groom, Madam Lu.
  • 学洙 - The name of the husband, Xuezhu.
  • 媒氏 - The matchmaker, a key figure in the story.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 学洙出之椟中 - Metaphorical expression meaning 'Xue Zhu emerged from the box,' symbolizing a hidden or protected state.
  • 其实少艳梅三岁 - Emphasizes the factual age difference, 'in reality, he is three years younger than Yan Mei.'
  • 而悬弧之斯 - Refers to the ancient custom of hanging a bow to celebrate a boy's birth.
  • 果在来朝 - Indicates a future event, 'it will indeed happen tomorrow.'
  • 乃知媪言非谬 - Means 'then it was known that the old woman's words were not false.'
  • 初犹冀其稍可 - Means 'initially, he still hoped it would be somewhat acceptable.'
  • 及就烛下推之 - Means 'when he examined her under the candlelight.'
  • 则视向之贫殀者，殆有甚焉 - Means 'then saw that the previously poor and frail one was even worse.'
  • 艳梅益泫然出涕 - Means 'Yan Mei wept even more tearfully.'
  • 究亦无可如何 - Means 'in the end, there was nothing to be done.'
  • 甫周岁，而学沫病矣 - Means 'just when he turned one year old, Xue Mo fell ill.'
  • 盖学洙体貌虽伟，实系童年 - Means 'because Xue Zhu was physically strong, but he was still a child.'
  • 燕尔既已，孔嘉新婚 - Means 'the joy of marriage had already passed, and the marriage was very good.'
  • 又值艳梅花月之态 - Means 'also coincided with Yan Mei's blooming and moonlit beauty.'
  • 尽足销魂 - Means 'enough to make one's soul melt,' describing intense beauty.
  • 斧斤之伐，能无蚀骨 - Means 'the cutting of axes, how could it not eat into the bones,' suggesting harsh impact.
  • 遂患瘵疾，卧床不起 - Means 'then fell ill with consumption and could not get up from bed.'
  • 初艳梅既知夫命，首虑其殀 - Means 'initially, Yan Mei, knowing her husband's fate, first worried about his death.'
  • 诸事撙节 - Means 'practiced frugality.'
  • 兼劝姑使之出外就傅 - Means 'also advised her mother-in-law to send him out to find a tutor.'
  • 虽不见听 - Means 'although not listened to.'
  • 家人皆称道其贤 - Means 'family members all praised her virtue.'
  • 至是危笃 - Means 'at this point, he was seriously ill.'
  • 乃窃自恫曰 - Means 'then secretly lamented.'
  • 非我死夫子，夫子实由我而疾 - Means 'not because I died, but because of me, my husband fell ill.'
  • 我则何心而忍于坐视 - Means 'what heart do I have to sit by and watch.'
  • 因自制疏文 - Means 'because of this, she wrote a petition.'
  • 乘夜密祷于上帝 - Means 'at night, secretly prayed to God.'
  • 其略曰 - Means 'its gist said.'
  • 宁为饿殍，同填沟壑于他年，不愿孀居，独守闺房于此日 - Means 'rather than be a starving corpse, to together fill the ditches in the future, do not want to be a widow, alone guard the inner room today.'
  • 情词悱恻 - Means 'the words were deeply moving.'
  • 约数十言 - Means 'about several dozen words.'
  • 祝讫，遽引刃自刲其臂 - Means 'after finishing the prayer, immediately drew the blade.'
  • 罗袂尽殷，香饥零落 - Means 'all the sleeves were dyed, fragrant blood scattered.'
  • 痛极，遂仆于地 - Means 'extremely painful, then fell to the ground.'
  • 迷惘中闻有人大呼曰 - Means 'in a daze, heard someone.'
  • 上帝有命，已许汝易夭而贫矣 - Means 'God has a command, has already allowed you to exchange early death for poverty.'
  • 声巨若雷，惊悸而苏 - Means 'the sound was as loud as thunder, startled and woke up.'
  • 人尚寂无知者 - Means 'people were still silent, no one knew.'

  • 学洙 - Name of a character in the story.
